gene_description: Cytochrome P450 2C9 — major hepatic enzyme responsible for oxidative metabolism of THC, phenytoin, NSAIDs, sulfonylureas, and warfarin. *2 and *3 alleles cause 30% and 80% reduction in activity, respectively.

source.title: Pharmacogenomics of CYP2C9: Functional and Clinical Considerations
source.finding: CYP2C9*2 (rs1799853) and *3 (rs1057910) reduce enzyme activity by 30% and 80% respectively. THC metabolism is significantly reduced in *3 carriers, leading to prolonged psychoactive effects. Allele frequencies up to 18% in Europeans.

TT.effect: Homozygous for CYP2C9*2 (Arg144Cys). Approximately 30% reduction in CYP2C9 activity. THC clearance is moderately decreased, potentially leading to prolonged psychoactive effects and elevated plasma levels. Also impacts metabolism of phenytoin, NSAIDs, and sulfonylureas.
TT.recommendation: Reduce THC/dronabinol doses and extend dosing intervals. For phenytoin, reduce dose by 25% per CPIC guidelines. For sulfonylureas (glimepiride, glipizide), start at lower doses and monitor blood glucose. Avoid high-frequency cannabis use.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carrier of CYP2C9*2. Mildly reduced CYP2C9 activity (~15% reduction). Slightly prolonged THC effects and modest impact on clearance of other CYP2C9 substrates.
TC.recommendation: Standard dosing generally appropriate for most drugs. May require modest dose adjustment for narrow therapeutic index drugs. Monitor clinical response.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carrier of CYP2C9*2. Mildly reduced CYP2C9 activity (~15% reduction). Slightly prolonged THC effects and modest impact on clearance of other CYP2C9 substrates.
CT.recommendation: Standard dosing generally appropriate for most drugs. May require modest dose adjustment for narrow therapeutic index drugs. Monitor clinical response.

CC.phenotype: Normal CYP2C9 Activity at This Locus
CC.effect: Wild-type at the *2 position. Normal CYP2C9 activity at this locus (still check *3 variant rs1057910 for complete phenotype assignment).
CC.recommendation: Standard dosing per clinical guidelines. Check CYP2C9*3 status for complete metabolizer phenotype.

CC.effect: Homozygous for CYP2C9*3 (Ile359Leu). Approximately 80% reduction in CYP2C9 activity. Dramatically prolonged THC psychoactive effects and significantly elevated plasma concentrations. High risk of adverse effects from phenytoin, warfarin, NSAIDs, and sulfonylureas due to impaired clearance.
CC.recommendation: Avoid THC/dronabinol or use minimal doses with close monitoring. For phenytoin, reduce dose by 50% per CPIC. For warfarin, reduce initial dose significantly. For sulfonylureas, use alternative diabetes medications. Closely monitor for drug toxicity.

CA.effect: Heterozygous carrier of CYP2C9*3. Approximately 40% reduction in CYP2C9 activity. Moderately prolonged THC effects. Clinically significant for drugs with narrow therapeutic indices.
CA.recommendation: Reduce THC doses and consider longer dosing intervals. For phenytoin, reduce dose by 25%. Monitor for enhanced drug effects and adverse reactions with all CYP2C9 substrates.

AC.effect: Heterozygous carrier of CYP2C9*3. Approximately 40% reduction in CYP2C9 activity. Moderately prolonged THC effects. Clinically significant for drugs with narrow therapeutic indices.
AC.recommendation: Reduce THC doses and consider longer dosing intervals. For phenytoin, reduce dose by 25%. Monitor for enhanced drug effects and adverse reactions with all CYP2C9 substrates.

AA.phenotype: Normal CYP2C9 Activity at This Locus
AA.effect: Wild-type at the *3 position. Normal CYP2C9 activity at this locus (still check *2 variant rs1799853 for complete phenotype assignment).
AA.recommendation: Standard dosing per clinical guidelines. Check CYP2C9*2 status for complete metabolizer phenotype.
